The '''R6-series astromech droid''' was the sixth [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech droid]] model made by [[Industrial Automaton/Legends|Industrial Automaton]] under their [[R-series/Legends|R-series]] of [[Droid/Legends|droids]].

Released soon after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], the R6 was IA's attempt at repairing their own reputation after the tremendous failure that was their [[R5-series astromech droid|R5 series]]. To prove that each new series beyond their famous [[R2-series astromech droid/Legends|R2-series]] would not be inferior, the designers of the R6 took their time in working out every problem and glitch that plagued the previous model. After years in planning, IA released the R6 model with obvious intent to try and recapture the hearts of their consumers.

==Description==
The R6-series [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech]] was similar in design to the [[R5-series astromech droid|R5-series]], although it incorporated a more traditional central [[Sensor/Legends|sensor]] and [[Holoprojector/Legends|holoprojector]] array. The R6 was an improvement over all the previously successful models in the [[R-series]], with none of the weaknesses that were the hallmark of the R5-series.<ref name="AEG" />

The R6 had a pleasant personality which was similar to those in displayed by the R2-series.<ref name="EGTD" /> They were loyal, helpful, bright, and cheerful. Highly skilled, R6 droids were equipped to carry out a variety of tasks.<ref name="AEG" /> Its [[Computer/Legends|computer]] processors were capable of storing up to twelve [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] jumps in active [[RAM|memory]].<ref name="EGTD" />

[[File:R6-R7_EGTD.jpg|thumb|left|180px|R6 and R7-series schematics]]
Standard equipment installed on the R6 included an [[Acoustic signaller/Legends|acoustic signaller]], [[Arc welder/Legends|arc welder]], [[Circular saw/Legends|circular saw]], a 0.3 [[Meter/Legends|meter]] long extendible [[video sensor]] with a full 360-degree rotation, a [[Fire extinguisher/Legends|fire extinguisher]], a [[Holoprojector/Legends|holographic projector]]/[[Holorecorder/Legends|recorder]], retractable [[Manipulator arm/Legends|fine worker arm]], retractable [[Grasper arm/Legends|heavy grasper arm]], and a [[Viewscreen/Legends|video display screen]].<ref name="J7" />

==History==
[[File:R6droid.jpg|thumb|right|180px|An R6-series [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech droid]]]]
In the wake of the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], as [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] was realizing they could make a fresh start under the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], Industrial Automaton took the opportunity to repair their corporate image which was still tarnished by the failure of the [[R5-series astromech droid]]. Industrial Automaton entered into business with the New Republic to produce a new astromech for a proposed [[E-7 E-wing multi-role starfighter|new]] [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]]. However, this project would take [[Standard year/Legends|years]] to come to fruition. In the interim, Industrial Automaton had been designing the R6-series for years with the company's [[Engineer/Legends|engineers]] working hard to solve all the bugs and issues that had plagued the release of the R5.<ref name="EGTD" />

Industrial Automaton released the R6-series to the public with large scale advertising campaign. Although more expensive than both the [[R4 astromech droid/Legends|R4]] and R5-series, it cost less than the original R2-series. The R6 was an instant success with customers buying them in droves,<ref name="EGTD" /> and the high demand led to them being sold out long before orders could be fulfilled.<ref name="AEG" /> The droid sold well<ref name="J7" /> until the [[Thrawn campaign|campaign]] led by [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]] shattered the galactic peace and prosperity.<ref name="EGTD" />
